verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted glandular kallikrein serine protease produced by prostate epithelium and released into seminal fluid and serum; it has a signal peptide but no TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display of the protein body; no narrow germline/developmental lineage with its own anchor (prostate restriction is secretory, not surface); not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool or cycling; no wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM partner. Clinical hK2-targeted programs (e.g. radioligands) bind the secreted protease pool, not a stable cell-surface form.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Some reports describe hK2 association with prostate cell surfaces via reversible interactions, but no wash-resistant TM-partner tether is established.
